summary: Pleasant audience‑captured tape that conveys Shoreline’s ambience, highlighted by a stellar Help > Slip > Franklin's sequence and a solid first set.
review: The recording offers a warm, ambient sound that lets listeners feel the vibe of a Shoreline night, while the performance shines with a very nice first set and an especially strong front half of the second set. The Help > Slip > Franklin's Tower suite is called out as one of the best since the revival, and the jam after Terrapin Station keeps the energy high even though the show tapers slightly later on.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews are 4‑star and praise both the music and the pleasant, ambient recording; the show is highlighted as solid with standout moments, while the tape’s sound quality is good but not described as pristine.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Pleasant, warm ambience that captures the reserved‑seating sound of Shoreline; clear enough to convey venue atmosphere with typical audience background noise.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: Nice first set followed by an extremely solid front half of the second set; a gorgeous space segment adds depth before the show dips slightly but remains enjoyable.
